narrative: Today's strongest signal comes from population and conservation biology: a three-decade study of Amazonian jaguars reveals a demographic shift toward male-biased populations despite high floodplain densities, while a global IUCN data-deficiency analysis pinpoints priority regions for extinction-risk monitoring. In climate-linked work, Rocky Mountain conifer recruitment is declining sharply under heat and drought even in heat-adapted populations, and reservoir methane emissions rose markedly in a drier year, underscoring feedback risks as climates dry. Landscape and fire themes intersect in new evidence that reproductive maturity, not climate alone, governs the persistence of an endangered obligate-seeder species, and that low-intensity agriculture can sustain ant-mediated seed dispersal in fragmented tropical dry-forest landscapes. On the methods front, several new tools broaden monitoring capacity, including an R package for inferring forest structure from remote sensing and an integrated acoustic-plus-point-count model for absolute abundance estimation. Evolutionary biology contributes a synthesis of what climate adaptation can learn from natural selection, alongside phylogenomic evidence that streptophyte multicellularity evolved through repeated, non-linear transitions rather than a single trajectory toward complexity. Biogeographic and flux studies round out the day, from invasive plant climatic-niche shifts in India to a review reframing nighttime vapor pressure deficit as an underappreciated driver of Northern Hemisphere vegetation productivity.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: Introduces a new framework combining eDNA, graph theory and niche modelling for ecosystem assessment.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Machine learning model estimates forest soil organic carbon density from multi-source data.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: New multiscale species distribution model reveals seascape niche partitioning among fish species.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Novel model integrates passive acoustic monitoring with point-count data for absolute abundance.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: BIMr2 is a new tool inferring migration rates and drivers from multilocus genotype data.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: New R package ForestForTrees infers forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing.

methods_radar.title: sampley: A Python package for sampling visual survey data
methods_radar.note: New Python package sampley standardises spatiotemporal sampling of visual survey data.

methods_radar.title: From video to behaviour: An LSTM‐based approach for automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild
methods_radar.note: LSTM-based deep learning framework enables automated nest behaviour recognition in the wild.

methods_radar.title: Mothbox and Mothbot: Automated light trap and data processing system for scalable insect monitoring
methods_radar.note: Mothbox/Mothbot are new open-source hardware and ML tools for scalable insect monitoring.

methods_radar.title: Cell‐based crop phenotyping for future climates
methods_radar.note: Proposes a robotics/ML pipeline for high-throughput cell-based crop phenotyping.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks species distribution model approaches using a loggerhead turtle case study.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Extends camera-trap monitoring vertically to capture arboreal-terrestrial community stratification.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Multi-satellite comparison benchmarks forest growth detection across karst and non-karst reserves.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Evaluates and addresses false positive/negative detection error sources in eDNA study design.

methods_radar.title: In situ aerial bioacoustic monitoring from extremely noisy drone recordings
methods_radar.note: Improves drone-based bioacoustic sensing to filter rotor self-noise from recordings.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to track wolf recolonisation across the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Uses trait data and camera-trap abundance indices to test the abundant-center hypothesis.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Applies Sentinel-2 imagery to delineate vegetation patches in post-industrial landscapes.

methods_radar.title: eDNA sampling reveals no evidence of negative effects of beaver recolonisation on the catchment‐scale distribution of migratory fish
methods_radar.note: Applies catchment-scale eDNA sampling to assess beaver-fish co-distribution.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Applies environmental DNA to resolve community-level genetic structure patterns.

methods_radar.title: Linking regional and global functional trait data: insights from mammal communities in a fragmented Atlantic Forest landscape
methods_radar.note: Tests whether a global trait database represents local fragmented-forest mammal community structure.
